What do you love most?: Everything is in one place finally. I stopped context-switching between 4 apps.
What's your biggest frustration?: Databases can be slow when they get big. Like 1000+ rows and it lags.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Absolutely. Already got two other teams using it.

What do you love most?: The templates are incredible. Saved me hours setting up our engineering wiki.
What's your biggest frustration?: Wish there were better code block features. Syntax highlighting is limited compared to dedicated tools.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes but with the caveat that it takes some time to set up well.

What do you love most?: Replaced our horrible Confluence setup. People actually READ the wiki now.
What's your biggest frustration?: Nothing major. Minor UI complaints — I wish I could customize the sidebar more.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Already recommended it to 3 other consulting firms. It's a game changer for us.

What do you love most?: It's good. Does what we need. Better than what we had before (Google Docs everywhere).
What's your biggest frustration?: The mobile app is terrible. Like really bad. I can barely use it on my phone.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Probably yes. Depends on what they need it for.

What do you love most?: Scale is impressive. 4500 people on it and it works. Security features gave our IT team confidence.
What's your biggest frustration?: Permissions model is confusing. Too many levels. Had to create a whole internal guide on who can see what.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes for large companies that need a knowledge platform. Not sure about small teams — might be overkill.

What do you love most?: It's fine for basic PM. We use it alongside Jira which is not ideal.
What's your biggest frustration?: Why can't I have recurring tasks natively? Had to build a workaround with automations. This should be basic.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Maybe. Honestly if you're heavily using Jira you'll have friction.

What do you love most?: Love the databases. We built our entire production pipeline tracking in Notion. So flexible.
What's your biggest frustration?: Learning curve is real. Took our team about a month to really get comfortable.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes — it's like a Swiss army knife for operations.

What do you love most?: The database views are perfect for content planning. Calendar view, board view — all connected to the same data.
What's your biggest frustration?: Notion AI is hit or miss. Sometimes the summaries are great. Sometimes it hallucinates stuff that's not in the doc.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes especially for content/media teams.

What do you love most?: Search with AI is incredible. I can ask questions about our entire codebase documentation and get real answers.
What's your biggest frustration?: Would love GitHub integration to be deeper. Right now we just link to PRs manually.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: 100%. Our eng team went from "I hate the wiki" to actually contributing regularly.

What do you love most?: Fine for managing my team's work. Not as powerful as dedicated PM tools but good enough.
What's your biggest frustration?: Can't do resource allocation or capacity planning well. Had to export to sheets for that.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes with caveats. Great for knowledge work. For heavy PM needs look elsewhere too.

What do you love most?: Database relations changed everything. We linked protocols to experiments to results. It's like a custom research tool.
What's your biggest frustration?: Offline mode doesn't work well. We work in labs without reliable wifi sometimes.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes especially for research/biotech teams that need structured documentation.

What do you love most?: Our store managers actually use this. Confluence was a ghost town. Notion is alive.
What's your biggest frustration?: Wish there was a way to restrict editing to specific sections of a page. Right now it's all-or-nothing per page.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Already recommended to our sister company. They're evaluating now.

What do you love most?: We built a mini CRM in Notion with databases. Works great for our scale. Saved us from buying HubSpot.
What's your biggest frustration?: Automations are limited. If I could trigger more complex workflows I wouldn't need Zapier.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes for small/mid teams. Enterprise might need something more robust.

What do you love most?: Rolling this out company-wide was easier than expected. The workspace structure makes sense for large orgs.
What's your biggest frustration?: Import from Confluence was painful. A lot of formatting broke. Took weeks to clean up.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes but warn them about migration. Plan for it. Don't think it'll be quick.

What do you love most?: It's ok. I use it because I have to. For my own tasks I prefer Todoist.
What's your biggest frustration?: It tries to do everything and doesn't excel at any one thing. Jack of all trades situation.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Not really. Most people here use it because it's mandated, not because they chose it.

What do you love most?: Template system is solid. We standardized our consulting deliverables and it saved a ton of time.
What's your biggest frustration?: Real-time collab still isn't as smooth as Google Docs. Occasional conflicts and weird cursor behavior.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes for teams that need templates and structured docs. Google Docs if you just need basic collab.

What do you love most?: Not much honestly. We went all-in and it was too much.
What's your biggest frustration?: Everything is a page and everything is a database and nothing has a clear purpose. It's overwhelmingly flexible. We spent more time organizing Notion than doing actual work.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: No. It's a productivity tool that destroys productivity if you're not careful. Get someone to set it up for you or don't bother.

What do you love most?: It's just pleasant to use. The design is beautiful. Writing in Notion feels good.
What's your biggest frustration?: The relationship between workspaces and pages and databases is confusing for new users. Took me 3 months to really understand the data model.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes! But set aside time for proper onboarding. It's not intuitive at first.

What do you love most?: Replaced Smartsheet for our PMO. Databases + timelines + docs in one place = exactly what we needed.
What's your biggest frustration?: Need better reporting/rollup features. For exec dashboards we still export to PowerPoint which is dumb.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Yes if you're willing to invest in setting it up right. The ROI is there but it takes 2-3 months to see it.

What do you love most?: The product is good but the pricing isn't. $18/user/month adds up fast when you have 400 people and half of them just need to READ docs.
What's your biggest frustration?: Pricing model penalizes large teams with lots of readers. We're paying full price for people who look at the wiki once a week. Need a viewer/editor split.
Would you recommend to a colleague? (ver: Mixed. The product is great, the pricing is hard to justify for everyone.
